articles.title: New Singapore space agency seeks to build up the country’s space industry
articles.summary:
Singapore’s new space agency signed a cooperation agreement with its Japanese counterpart as part of its efforts to build up the country’s space industry.
The post New Singapore space agency seeks to build up the country’s space industry appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Live coverage: SpaceX to launch Falcon 9 rocket on record-breaking 36th flight
articles.summary: The flight-leading booster, B1067, will launch the Starlink 10-42 mission from Cape Canaveral Space Force Station. Liftoff from pad 40 is scheduled for 5:25 a.m. EDT (0925 UTC) on Thursday morning.

articles.title: ispace to send larger payloads to the moon on SpaceX’s Starship
articles.summary:
Japanese lunar exploration company ispace is buying space on a future Starship lunar lander mission to deliver larger payloads to the moon.
The post ispace to send larger payloads to the moon on SpaceX’s Starship appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Students Connect NASA Science With Indigenous Knowledge to Study Coastal Erosion
articles.summary: Story by Keri Moskowitz, Gulf of Maine Research Institute For the Pleasant Point Passamaquoddy Reservation, or Sipayik, the ocean has always been a teacher. Situated in what is known as Downeast Maine, along the shores of Passamaquoddy Bay, generations of Indigenous people have lived along the coast, learning from the tides, the land, and their […]

articles.title: Loft Orbital Books Multiple Launches Aboard MaiaSpace Rockets
articles.summary:
US-based satellite operator Loft Orbital has signed a multi-launch agreement with ArianeGroup subsidiary MaiaSpace. Although the announcement provided few details, it did share that the first flight was expected in 2028. In an 8 July press release, MaiaSpace explained that the multi-launch agreement “consolidates its launch manifest,” adding that the company has now sold more […]
The post Loft Orbital Books Multiple Launches Aboard MaiaSpace Rockets appeared first on European Spaceflight.

articles.title: Hubble Captures Star-Studded Cluster
articles.summary: This image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope showcases Messier 3 (M3), one of the Milky Way galaxy’s most massive globular clusters, or spherical collections of gravitationally bound stars. Globular clusters are made up of ancient stars that formed at roughly the same time from the same cloud of gas, giving those stars similar ages. Around 150 known globular clusters are […]
